summary.intent: A user reported that LM Studio and llama.cpp local model options were missing from the onboarding LLM selection UI in moltis.
summary.outcome: Added `lmstudio` to the default `offered` list in the config template and promoted both `local-llm` and `lmstudio` to the `RECOMMENDED_PROVIDERS` set in the onboarding JS, making both local inference options visible in the main onboarding section. Changes were committed and pushed.

repo: Known providers are defined in `known_providers.rs` but visibility during onboarding is controlled by two separate gates: the `offered` array in `crates/config/src/template.rs` and the `RECOMMENDED_PROVIDERS` set in the JS onboarding view.
Providers in `offered` but not in `RECOMMENDED_PROVIDERS` are buried under an 'All providers' collapsible dropdown rather than shown in the main recommended section.
lmstudio was a fully configured known provider (endpoint, auth type) that was simply never wired into the onboarding flow.

code.finding: RECOMMENDED_PROVIDERS set controls which providers appear in the main onboarding section vs. the collapsed 'All providers' dropdown. Did not include local-llm or lmstudio before this fix.
Default `offered` array controls which providers are visible during onboarding at all. lmstudio was missing; local-llm was present but not recommended.

workflow: Issues are tracked in an internal 'beads' system, not GitHub — GH issues are optional.
Biome is used for JS linting/formatting; clean check required before committing JS changes.
Schema tests for the `offered` list only validate parsing with a single entry, not the full default list contents — so adding providers to the default list doesn't require test updates.

friction: Two separate configuration points (Rust template + JS RECOMMENDED_PROVIDERS) must both be updated to fully surface a provider during onboarding — this split is non-obvious and caused the gap.
open_items: The commented-out 'All available providers' reference list in template.rs also needed lmstudio added — easy to miss when adding new providers.
No automated test validates that the default `offered` list matches the JS RECOMMENDED_PROVIDERS set, leaving room for future drift.
